title: CollateralAssessmentTypeValues
description:
Applying a safety discount to the collaterals market value to account for price volatility or risk.

Comment:
Example:
A stock worth $100,000 might be accepted with a 20% haircut, meaning only $80,000 counts toward securing a loan.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Valuation Assessment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Evaluation of the current market value of collateral to ensure it sufficiently covers the exposure it secures.

Example:
A bank receives government bonds as collateral for a loan. The bank marks them to market daily; if the bonds are worth $1,000,000 but the loan exposure is $1,200,000, additional collateral may be requested.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Eligibility Assessment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Checking if the collateral meets contractual or regulatory requirements (type, rating, currency, ownership).

Comment:
Example:
A repo agreement allows only AAA-rated corporate bonds as collateral. If the borrower pledges BBB-rated bonds, they are ineligible.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Concentration Assessment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Evaluating whether there is over-reliance on a single type of collateral or a single issuer, to manage risk.

Comment:
Example:
A lender holds $10M of collateral, $8M of which is from a single corporate issuer. This triggers a concentration assessment to ensure the lender is not overly exposed if that issuer defaults.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Liquidity Assessment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Determining how easily and quickly collateral can be converted into cash at minimal loss.

Comment:
Example:
Cash and government bonds are highly liquid, while commercial real estate or private equity is less liquid. If a borrower defaults, liquid assets are preferred for immediate settlement.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Credit Assessment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Assessing the financial stability and creditworthiness of the entity providing the collateral.

Comment:
Example:
A borrower pledges corporate bonds, but the issuing company has a high default risk. The bank may require a higher collateral amount or reject it entirely.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Documentation Assessment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Ensuring the collateral is legally enforceable and properly documented under applicable law.

Comment
Example:
Verifying that pledged real estate has a lien correctly registered in the land registry so the lender can seize it if the borrower defaults.
